name: AI-Progress v2: Self-Evolving Intelligence System
description: Transform this dashboard from a static tracker into a living system that researches, learns, proposes, and acts autonomously. Multi-AI orchestration where the system picks the best AI for each task.
currentState: v0.4 — 46 tools tracked, 6 tabs, JSON data, manual updates
targetState: Self-updating system that discovers tools before you hear about them, tests them automatically, and briefs you on what matters

phases.name: Multi-AI Data Pipeline
phases.description: Connect to multiple AI APIs (Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, Perplexity) to cross-verify information. Each AI has strengths — Perplexity for real-time search, Claude for analysis, GPT for breadth, Gemini for Google ecosystem data.
tasks: Create API integration layer (Python scripts in /scripts)
Map each AI's free tier limits and API costs
Build cross-verification: ask 3 AIs the same question, flag disagreements
Auto-select best AI per task type (research → Perplexity, analysis → Claude, code → Claude Code)
Store API responses in data/ai-responses/ for audit trail
phases.estimatedCost: Free tier covers most — Claude free API, GPT free tier, Gemini free, Perplexity free searches
workarounds: Rotate between AIs to stay within limits
Cache responses to avoid redundant calls
Use browser automation as fallback when APIs hit limits

phases.name: Autonomous Research Agent
phases.description: Scheduled system that runs daily: scans news sources, checks for new tools, verifies pricing, detects shutdowns, and generates a daily briefing.
tasks: Claude scheduled task: daily research sweep
Web scraping fallback for sources without APIs
Auto-generate new test entries when tools launch
Dead tool detection (check URLs, look for shutdown announcements)
Daily briefing generation: 'Here's what changed in AI today'
Auto-commit data changes to GitHub with detailed diffs
phases.estimatedCost: Free (Claude scheduled tasks + GitHub Actions free tier)
workarounds: Use RSS feeds where available
Use AI to read pages via browser automation
Perplexity API as search proxy

phases.name: Browser Automation Testing
phases.description: AI opens browser, navigates to tools, runs comparison tests, takes screenshots, generates reports. Uses Claude's MCP browser tools or Puppeteer.
tasks: Auto-open tool URLs and capture screenshots
Run side-by-side comparisons (same prompt to 3 tools, compare outputs)
CAPTCHA handling via browser automation tools
Screenshot comparison: visual diff of tool outputs
Generate test report cards automatically
Video recording of test sessions for review
phases.estimatedCost: Free (runs on your Windows PC)
workarounds: Store session cookies
Use tools' APIs instead of UI where available
AI vision to solve simple CAPTCHAs

phases.name: Predictive Intelligence
phases.description: Use historical data + AI reasoning to predict: which tools will dominate, which will die, what new categories will emerge, what you should learn next.
tasks: Build historical data tracking (weekly snapshots of rankings/pricing)
Trend analysis: trajectory of each tool over time
Predict shutdowns (signs: price hikes, declining updates, competitor surge)
Predict breakouts (signs: rapid user growth, funding announcements, viral demos)
Personalized recommendations based on your test history and interests
Weekly 'AI Forecast' briefing
phases.estimatedCost: Free (analysis on local data)
blockers: Need historical data first — takes weeks of collection
workarounds: Backfill from web archives where possible
Start collecting now, predictions improve over time

biggestRisk: API rate limits on free tiers
mitigation: Multi-AI rotation, aggressive caching, browser automation fallback

name: Idea Machine: AI Virtual Assistant
description: A personal AI assistant app that lives on your phone, connects to your computer remotely, and can DO things — not just answer questions. Generates images, videos, programs, websites. Talks to you via voice. Orchestrates multiple AIs behind the scenes.
targetState: Smartphone app with voice interface that connects to a home server running AI orchestration, capable of generating any digital content on command

phases.name: Voice-First Local Server
phases.description: Set up a local server on your Windows PC that accepts voice commands via a web app. Uses text-to-speech (ElevenLabs or free alternatives) and speech-to-text (Whisper). Accessible from phone via local network or tunnel.
tasks: Install Python + FastAPI on Windows PC as local server
Integrate Whisper (free, local) for speech-to-text
Integrate TTS: ElevenLabs API (free tier: limited) or Coqui TTS (free, local, unlimited)
Create mobile-friendly web interface (PWA — installable on phone)
Set up Cloudflare Tunnel or Tailscale for remote access from phone outside home
Basic conversation loop: speak → transcribe → AI processes → TTS response
phases.estimatedCost: Free (Whisper is free, Coqui TTS is free, Cloudflare tunnel is free)
workarounds: Wake-on-LAN to start PC remotely
Tailscale for zero-config VPN
Fallback to cloud TTS when local unavailable

phases.name: Multi-AI Orchestrator
phases.description: The assistant routes tasks to the best AI automatically. Image request → Midjourney/DALL-E. Code request → Claude Code. Research → Perplexity. Music → Suno. The user just describes what they want.
tasks: Task classifier: analyze user intent and route to best AI
API integrations: OpenAI (images + chat), Claude (analysis + code), Perplexity (search), Suno (music), Runway (video)
Fallback chains: if primary AI fails or hits limit, try next best
Result aggregation: combine outputs from multiple AIs into one response
Cost tracking: monitor API usage, prefer free tiers, warn before paid calls
Plugin architecture: easy to add new AI tools as they launch
phases.estimatedCost: Mostly free tiers — paid APIs only for heavy use ($5-20/month estimated)
workarounds: Browser automation for tools without APIs
Queue system to respect rate limits
Local models (Stable Diffusion, DeepSeek) as unlimited fallbacks

phases.name: Content Generation Engine
phases.description: Generate any digital content on demand: images, videos, music, websites, programs, documents. All from voice commands.
tasks: Image generation pipeline: prompt → Stable Diffusion (local, free) or DALL-E API
Video generation: Runway/Kling API or local Stable Video Diffusion
Music generation: Suno API or Udio API
Website generation: Claude Code generates HTML/CSS/JS, auto-deploys to GitHub Pages
Program generation: describe what you want → AI writes, tests, and packages it
Document generation: reports, presentations, spreadsheets from voice description
phases.estimatedCost: Local generation is free. API calls: $0-20/month depending on usage
blockers: GPU needed for local image/video generation
workarounds: Use cloud APIs when local GPU insufficient
Chain multiple AIs for quality improvement
Human review step for important outputs

phases.name: Autonomous Agent Mode
phases.description: The assistant doesn't just respond — it proactively suggests, plans, and executes multi-step projects. It can install software, configure tools, and manage its own capabilities.
tasks: Self-installation: assistant can pip install packages, download models, configure APIs
Project planning: break big ideas into steps, estimate time/cost, execute sequentially
Inter-AI conversations: one AI reviews another AI's output for quality
Memory system: remember past conversations, preferences, project context
Proactive suggestions: 'I noticed a new image AI launched — want me to test it?'
Error recovery: when something fails, automatically try alternative approaches
phases.estimatedCost: Varies by usage — mostly free with occasional API costs
workarounds: Confirmation prompts for destructive actions
Sandbox environments for testing
Rollback capability for every action

biggestRisk: Scope — this is a massive project
mitigation: Phase 1 is achievable in days. Each phase works standalone. Build incrementally.

name: Boundary Pusher: What's Actually Possible Right Now
description: A systematic effort to find the actual limits of current AI by combining tools in ways nobody has tried. Not theoretical — actually do it, document what works, document what fails, and publish the results.
currentState: Concept — we know individual tool capabilities but not combined limits
targetState: A living document of 'what AI can actually do today' based on real experiments, with auto-detection of when limits expand

phases.name: Capability Mapping
phases.description: Systematically test what each AI tool can and cannot do. Not marketing claims — actual tested limits. Document failure modes.
tasks: Create standardized test suite for each category (text, image, video, code, audio)
Run identical prompts across all tools, score results
Document exact failure points: where does each tool break?
Test edge cases: very long inputs, complex instructions, multi-step reasoning
Test combination workflows: output of one AI as input to another
Publish results as structured data in this dashboard
phases.estimatedCost: Free (using free tiers of all tools)
workarounds: Automate testing via browser automation
Prioritize most impactful comparisons first

phases.name: Combination Experiments
phases.description: What happens when you chain 5 AIs together? Claude writes a script → GPT reviews it → Gemini tests it → Perplexity fact-checks the output → Claude summarizes. Find powerful combinations nobody has tried.
tasks: Design 20 multi-AI pipeline experiments
Build automated pipeline runner (input → AI1 → AI2 → AI3 → output)
Test creative chains: Claude writes lyrics → Suno generates song → ElevenLabs creates voice intro
Test code chains: Claude Code writes app → GPT writes tests → Gemini reviews security
Test research chains: Perplexity searches → Claude analyzes → GPT generates report → Claude critiques
Score each pipeline: quality, speed, cost, reliability
phases.estimatedCost: Mostly free tiers — some API costs for heavy chains
workarounds: Start with 2-tool chains, scale up
Error handling at each stage
Human checkpoint for expensive operations

phases.name: Impossible Task Attempts
phases.description: Take tasks that are 'impossible' today and throw everything at them. Full movie from a text prompt. Complete business plan with financial projections. Functional mobile app from a sentence. See how close we get.
tasks: Define 10 'impossible' challenges with clear success criteria
Challenge 1: Generate a 5-minute short film with consistent characters, plot, and soundtrack — entirely AI-generated
Challenge 2: Build and deploy a functional mobile app from a one-sentence description
Challenge 3: Create a complete online course (video, quizzes, slides) on any topic in under 1 hour
Challenge 4: Generate a 50-page research report with verified citations on a novel topic
Challenge 5: Create an original album (10 tracks, cover art, liner notes) in one session
Document exactly where each attempt succeeds, fails, and what would fix it
Re-attempt monthly as tools improve — track progress toward 'impossible'
phases.estimatedCost: Variable — some challenges may require paid tiers
workarounds: Human-in-the-loop for quality gaps
Mix AI and manual steps, document the ratio
Track which steps will be automatable next

phases.name: Publish & Automate Discovery
phases.description: Automatically re-run capability tests when tools update. Detect when a previously 'impossible' task becomes possible. Alert you immediately.
tasks: Automated regression testing of all tool capabilities (weekly)
Version tracking: when tools update, re-run relevant tests
Breakthrough detection: flag when test scores improve significantly
Public results page: share findings so others benefit
Community contribution: accept test submissions from others
Monthly 'State of AI Capability' report — auto-generated
phases.estimatedCost: Free (automated via GitHub Actions + scheduled tasks)
blockers: Scale — lots of tests to maintain
workarounds: Prioritize high-impact tests
AI generates and maintains test scripts

biggestRisk: Scope creep — there are infinite things to test
mitigation: Strict prioritization: test what matters most to YOU first. Expand from there.

name: Text to Physical Object: AI 3D Design → Print Pipeline
description: Go from a text description to a physical 3D-printed object using only AI tools. No CAD skills. Test every tool in the chain, document what works, what breaks, and build the most reliable pipeline possible. Then extend to mechanical parts, assemblies, and eventually functional products.
currentState: Research complete — Meshy (97% slicer pass, 55% watertight), Zoo (only parametric CAD AI), PrintPal (print-optimized). No single tool does it end-to-end reliably.
targetState: Reliable pipeline: describe an object → AI generates print-ready model → auto-repair → slice → send to printer. Works for both organic (figurines) and mechanical (gears, enclosures) parts.

phases.name: Tool Testing: Organic Models
phases.description: Test Meshy, Tripo, PrintPal with identical prompts. Print the results. Score: mesh quality, watertight percentage, print success rate, time to printable file.
tasks: Create 10 standardized test prompts (figurine, vase, phone case, chess piece, etc.)
Generate each prompt in Meshy, Tripo, PrintPal — document results
Export STL/3MF from each, run through mesh analysis (Blender 3D Print Toolbox)
Score watertight %, non-manifold edges, polygon count, file size
Slice in Bambu Studio/OrcaSlicer — note any errors
Print top 3 results, photograph and document quality
Build comparison database in data/3d-tests.json
phases.estimatedCost: Free tiers + filament cost (~$5-10 in PLA per test batch)
workarounds: Use online print services (JLCPCB 3D printing, Shapeways) if no local printer
Rotate between tools to stay within free limits

phases.name: Tool Testing: Mechanical Parts
phases.description: Test Zoo's text-to-CAD for mechanical parts that need dimensional accuracy. Can AI generate a gear, enclosure, bracket that actually works?
tasks: Create 10 mechanical test prompts (gear, enclosure, bracket, hinge, snap-fit, threaded bolt)
Generate in Zoo (KCL/API) — the only parametric CAD AI
Export STEP files, open in Fusion 360 or FreeCAD
Measure dimensional accuracy vs specification
Print mechanical parts, test fit and function
Compare Zoo output to manual CAD for same parts
Document where AI CAD fails vs succeeds
phases.estimatedCost: Zoo API: ~$5-10 for testing. FreeCAD is free.
blockers: Zoo is still early — complex parts may fail
workarounds: Use Zoo for initial geometry, manual CAD cleanup for tolerances
Backflip AI for mesh-to-parametric conversion if needed

phases.name: Automated Pipeline
phases.description: Build a script that chains: text prompt → AI generation → mesh repair → slicing → G-code output. Minimal human intervention.
tasks: Python script: send prompt to Meshy/Zoo API
Auto-repair: Blender Python API for manifold fixes
Auto-scale: detect model size, scale to target dimensions
Auto-slice: OrcaSlicer CLI for G-code generation
Quality check: automated mesh analysis before slicing
Output: print-ready G-code with estimated time and material
Web interface: paste description → get G-code
phases.estimatedCost: API costs only — ~$0.50-2 per model
workarounds: Conservative repair settings (better to over-repair than under)
Printer profile presets for common setups

phases.name: Full Product Pipeline
phases.description: Extend beyond single parts: multi-part assemblies, functional products, custom packaging, and direct-to-manufacturer ordering.
tasks: Multi-part assembly: AI generates parts that fit together
Tolerance management: automatic clearance for moving parts
Material selection: AI recommends PLA/PETG/TPU based on function
Color/texture: multi-material printing support
Direct ordering: API integration with JLCPCB 3D printing or Shapeways
Cost estimation: material + print time + shipping
Product listing: auto-generate product photos and descriptions
phases.estimatedCost: Variable — $1-50 per product depending on complexity and manufacturing
blockers: Multi-part assemblies are hard — tolerance stacking
No AI handles assemblies well yet
workarounds: Start with simple 2-part assemblies
Human review for critical dimensions
Iterate: print, measure, adjust, reprint

biggestRisk: Mesh quality — 45% of AI-generated meshes aren't watertight
mitigation: Automated repair pipeline + multiple generation attempts + quality scoring before printing

name: AI Electronics Lab: Text to PCB to Manufactured Board
description: Design electronic circuits using AI — from text description through schematic, PCB layout, to ordering manufactured boards from JLCPCB. Test every AI EDA tool, find the gaps, and bridge them. Target: a non-engineer should be able to describe what they want and get a working PCB.
currentState: Research complete — Quilter (layout, free beta), Flux.ai (full ECAD), CircuitMind (schematic from block diagram), DeepPCB (routing). No end-to-end text-to-PCB pipeline exists. Analog/RF/power design has ZERO AI tools.
targetState: Describe a circuit in plain English → AI generates schematic → AI layouts PCB → auto-validate against manufacturer DRC → one-click order from JLCPCB/PCBWay. Initially for digital circuits, eventually analog.

phases.name: Tool Testing: Simple Digital Boards
phases.description: Test Quilter, Flux.ai, DeepPCB with identical simple projects (LED blinker, USB-C breakout, ESP32 dev board). Score: time, quality, DRC pass rate, ordering readiness.
tasks: Design 5 test projects of increasing complexity (1-layer through 4-layer)
Test each in Quilter, Flux.ai, DeepPCB — document results and time
Run DRC against JLCPCB manufacturing rules
Export Gerbers from each tool, verify in online Gerber viewer
Order 1-2 boards from JLCPCB to verify manufacturability
Solder and test: does the board actually work?
Build comparison database in data/pcb-tests.json
phases.estimatedCost: Free tool tiers + ~$5-15 per PCB order from JLCPCB (minimum 5 boards)
workarounds: JLCPCB offers SMT assembly service
Start with through-hole components (easier to solder)
Use EasyEDA (free, integrated with JLCPCB) as baseline comparison

phases.name: Schematic Generation: CircuitMind + LLM
phases.description: Test CircuitMind ACE for schematic generation from block diagrams. Use Claude/GPT to generate the block diagrams from text descriptions. Bridge the gap.
tasks: Use Claude to convert text requirements into block diagrams
Feed block diagrams to CircuitMind ACE
Evaluate component selection quality and cost optimization
Test handoff: CircuitMind schematic → Quilter/Flux layout
Document where the chain breaks (analog, power, RF)
Build LLM prompt templates for electronics requirements
phases.estimatedCost: CircuitMind pricing TBD + Claude API (~$1-5 per session)
blockers: Handoff between tools requires manual steps
workarounds: Design power sections manually in KiCad (free)
Use LLMs to generate KiCad schematic files for analog portions
Human review at every handoff point

phases.name: The Analog Gap: Can We Bridge It?
phases.description: Analog/RF/power design has NO AI tools. This is the biggest gap in electronics AI. Can we use LLMs + SPICE simulation to partially fill it?
tasks: Test Claude/GPT ability to generate SPICE netlists for common circuits
Run LLM-generated circuits in LTspice (free) and evaluate correctness
Build a library of verified analog building blocks (voltage regulators, amplifiers, filters)
Test if LLMs can select appropriate components from datasheets
Explore: can we train a custom model on open-source analog designs?
Document exactly what analog AI CAN'T do — this is the research gap
phases.estimatedCost: LTspice is free. API costs minimal for testing.
blockers: Analog design is fundamentally harder for AI — noise, parasitics, non-idealities
workarounds: Start with low-power, low-frequency circuits where mistakes are safe
Cross-verify between multiple LLMs
Always simulate before manufacturing

phases.name: One-Click Manufacturing
phases.description: Automate the ordering process: AI validates design, generates manufacturing files, estimates cost, and places order with JLCPCB/PCBWay API.
tasks: JLCPCB API integration for automated ordering
Auto-generate BOM with verified in-stock components
Cost estimation: PCB fabrication + components + assembly + shipping
Design-for-manufacturing checks (minimum trace width, drill sizes, etc.)
Assembly file generation (pick-and-place, BOM mapping)
Tracking: order status, delivery estimation
Full pipeline: text description → manufactured board in mailbox within 2 weeks
phases.estimatedCost: PCB orders: $5-50 depending on complexity. Assembly: $20-200.
blockers: Assembly errors from AI-generated pick-and-place files
workarounds: Use JLCPCB's verified component library
Order extra boards for testing
Start with simple 2-layer boards

biggestRisk: AI cannot design analog circuits — this limits what boards can be fully automated
mitigation: Start with digital-only boards. Add manual analog sections as needed. Track when analog AI tools appear.

name: Workplace Automation: AI Handles Your Daily Computer Tasks
description: Systematically identify, analyze, and automate daily workplace computer tasks. Not just 'use ChatGPT' — actually observe what you do, map the workflows, find where AI can take over, build the automations, and measure time saved. Start with observation, end with an AI that handles routine work autonomously.
currentState: Concept — need to identify actual daily tasks first
targetState: AI assistant that knows your workflows, handles routine tasks autonomously, and proposes optimizations for complex ones. Phone answering, email triage, document generation, data entry — all AI-handled.

phases.name: Task Discovery & Mapping
phases.description: Before automating anything, we need to know what you actually do. No giant prompt needed — AI observes and maps. Use screen recording + AI analysis, or just a structured interview.
tasks: Option A: Install a time-tracking tool (RescueTime, Toggl — free tiers) for 1 week
Option B: Claude interviews you about your daily tasks (15 min structured session)
Option C: Screen recording of a typical day → AI analyzes the workflow
Map every task: what software, how often, how long, how repetitive
Categorize: fully automatable / partially automatable / requires human judgment
Identify the top 10 time-wasters that AI could handle
Create data/workplace-tasks.json with the complete task map
phases.estimatedCost: Free (all tools have free tiers)
blockers: Privacy: screen recording captures sensitive work data
Need your cooperation for accurate mapping
workarounds: Option B avoids screen recording entirely
Task mapping can be done conversationally
Start with non-sensitive tasks only

phases.name: Quick Wins: Immediate Automation
phases.description: Automate the easiest, most repetitive tasks first. Email templates, file organization, meeting summaries, form filling. These are solved problems — just need setup.
tasks: Email: auto-draft replies for common patterns (Outlook + Power Automate or Gmail + Zapier)
Calendar: AI schedules meetings, resolves conflicts, sends reminders
Documents: template generation for common reports/emails (Claude API + templates)
File organization: auto-sort downloads, rename files by content
Meeting notes: auto-transcribe and summarize (Otter.ai, Whisper local)
Data entry: AI reads documents and fills forms/spreadsheets
Browser automation: auto-fill forms, auto-login, auto-download reports
phases.estimatedCost: Zapier free tier (100 tasks/mo), Make free tier (1000 ops/mo), Otter.ai free (300 min/mo)
blockers: Workplace IT policies may restrict tool installation
workarounds: Use browser-based tools that don't need installation
Portable Python scripts that run without admin
Start with personal tasks, expand to work tasks after proving value

phases.name: AI Phone Assistant
phases.description: AI answers your phone, transcribes the call, looks up relevant information, drafts a response or takes action. This already exists in pieces — we need to connect them.
tasks: Speech-to-text: Whisper (free, local) or Google Speech API
Intent detection: Claude/GPT analyzes what the caller wants
Information lookup: search your files, CRM, knowledge base for relevant data
Response generation: AI drafts a response or escalates to you
Text-to-speech: ElevenLabs (limited free) or Coqui TTS (free, local, unlimited)
Phone integration: Twilio ($1/mo + per-minute) for actual phone connectivity
Voicemail alternative: AI takes the call, texts you a summary with suggested action
Full loop: caller speaks → AI understands → looks up info → responds → logs the call
phases.estimatedCost: Twilio: ~$1/mo + $0.013/min. Whisper: free. TTS: free (Coqui) or $5/mo (ElevenLabs)
blockers: Latency — caller may notice AI delay
workarounds: Start as voicemail replacement (no real-time pressure)
AI asks clarifying questions to buy time
Escalation: 'Let me connect you with Cole' for complex calls

phases.name: Deep Automation: Custom AI Agents
phases.description: Build custom AI agents for your specific workflows. An agent that knows your job, your tools, your preferences, and can execute multi-step tasks autonomously.
tasks: Build task-specific agents using Claude API or OpenAI Assistants API
Agent memory: store your preferences, past decisions, common patterns
Multi-step execution: agent completes 5-step task from single instruction
Tool integration: agent can use your software (browser automation, API calls)
Proactive suggestions: 'You usually send this report on Fridays — want me to draft it?'
Error handling: agent detects when it's unsure and asks you instead of guessing
Dashboard: track what the agent did, time saved, accuracy
Continuous improvement: agent learns from your corrections
phases.estimatedCost: API costs: $5-30/month depending on usage volume
blockers: Trust — hard to trust AI with important work tasks initially
Error rate needs to be very low
workarounds: Start in 'suggest mode' — AI proposes, you approve
Gradual autonomy: approve first 10 times, then auto-execute
Undo capability for every action

biggestRisk: Workplace IT policies may restrict what tools can be installed
mitigation: Start with personal workflow automation. Use browser-based and portable tools. Prove ROI before requesting IT exceptions.

name: Solo AI Film Studio: Complete Video Production
description: Produce a complete short film — script, storyboard, visuals, video, voice acting, music, sound effects, editing, color grading — using only AI tools. Zero camera, zero crew, zero budget. Two AI films premiered at Sundance 2026. A solo creator got a World Records India certification. This is real and happening now.
currentState: Proven possible — Sundance 2026 films, World Records India certified AI music video, MIT AI Film Hack showing 3-year progression. Pipeline: ChatGPT→Midjourney→Runway→ElevenLabs→Descript.
targetState: Repeatable pipeline that produces watchable 5-10 minute short films. Documented quality at each stage. Published on YouTube. Potentially monetizable.

phases.name: 60-Second Test Film
phases.description: Produce a 60-second short using the proven pipeline. Learn the tools, find the bottlenecks, document the quality.
tasks: Script: ChatGPT or Claude writes a simple 60-second narrative
Storyboard: Midjourney or FLUX generates key frames
Video: Runway Gen-4 or Kling animates storyboard frames
Voice: ElevenLabs generates narration/dialogue
Music: Suno generates background score
Sound effects: ElevenLabs Sound Effects or Freesound.org
Edit: Descript or DaVinci Resolve (free) for assembly
Grade: Colourlab AI for color correction
Total time target: under 4 hours for 60 seconds
phases.estimatedCost: Free tiers of most tools. Midjourney: ~$14 CAD/mo minimum.
workarounds: Narration-driven (no lip sync needed)
Consistent character via Midjourney style reference
Short cuts (2-3 sec) to hide temporal artifacts

phases.name: 5-Minute Short Film
phases.description: Scale up to a real short film with plot, characters, dialogue. Apply lessons from Phase 1.
tasks: Multi-character script with dialogue and plot arc
Character sheets: consistent character design across all scenes
Shot list: 40-60 shots with camera angles and transitions
Generate, assemble, and polish all elements
Sound design: layered audio (dialogue + music + SFX + ambience)
Publish to YouTube, measure audience response
Document total time and cost
phases.estimatedCost: $20-50 in AI tool subscriptions for one month
blockers: Character consistency over 5 minutes is much harder
workarounds: Mixed media: combine AI animation with motion graphics
Use consistent art style (anime, watercolor) to mask inconsistencies

biggestRisk: Character consistency — AI can't maintain the same character across many scenes
mitigation: Use style references, character sheets, and choose art styles that are more forgiving of variation (illustration, anime, abstract).

name: Medical AI Research Assistant: Deep Condition Analysis
description: Use AI to do deep research on specific medical conditions — not just search, but systematic literature analysis, treatment comparison, clinical trial tracking, imaging analysis, and finding specialists. Example: given 'pancreatic cancer', AI should find the latest treatments, experimental therapies, relevant clinical trials, AI tools that can analyze scans, and produce a comprehensive research package you can bring to a doctor.
currentState: AI can search and summarize medical information (Perplexity, Claude, GPT). Specialized medical AI tools exist but are fragmented: radiology AI (Aidoc, Viz.ai), drug discovery AI (Insilico Medicine), clinical trial matching. No unified research assistant combines them.
targetState: Given a condition name, produce a comprehensive research package: latest treatment options, experimental therapies, relevant clinical trials accepting patients, AI imaging tools for that condition, top specialists, patient communities, and a timeline of recent research breakthroughs. Updated weekly.

phases.name: Multi-Source Medical Literature Search
phases.description: Go beyond Google. Systematic search across PubMed, Google Scholar, clinical trial registries, preprint servers, and patient communities. Use multiple AIs to cross-verify medical claims.
tasks: PubMed API integration: search for recent papers on any condition
ClinicalTrials.gov API: find active trials accepting patients by condition + location
Google Scholar scraping: find cited reviews and meta-analyses
Perplexity: real-time search with source citations for latest developments
Claude + GPT: analyze and summarize papers at different reading levels
Cross-verify: run claims through multiple AIs, flag disagreements
Create structured output: evidence quality ratings for each finding
Output: comprehensive report with sections for patient, for doctor, for researcher
phases.estimatedCost: APIs mostly free for research-level queries. PubMed: free. ClinicalTrials.gov: free.
blockers: Medical accuracy is critical — AI hallucinations could be dangerous
workarounds: Always cite primary sources — never present AI summary as medical advice
Use Unpaywall API for free paper access
Cross-verify EVERY claim with at least 2 sources
Include disclaimer: this is research aid, not medical advice

phases.name: Medical Imaging AI Discovery
phases.description: For a given condition, find which AI tools can analyze relevant scans (MRI, CT, PET, pathology slides). If tools don't exist for this condition, identify research models that could be adapted.
tasks: Map existing FDA-approved AI radiology tools by condition (Aidoc, Viz.ai, Paige AI, PathAI)
Map research models: papers where AI achieved diagnostic accuracy for this condition
For pancreatic cancer specifically: find pancreatic mass detection AI, IPMN classification AI
Identify open-source models on Hugging Face for medical imaging
Assessment: which tools are clinically available vs research-only vs theoretical
If no tool exists: identify the gap, find relevant training datasets (TCIA, Kaggle medical)
Document how to request AI-assisted analysis from radiology departments
phases.estimatedCost: Research only — no cost beyond API queries
blockers: Most medical AI is clinical-only (not available to patients)
workarounds: Focus on what you can bring to your doctor as questions
Identify clinical trials USING imaging AI that you could enroll in
Open-source models can be explored for educational purposes

phases.name: Treatment Landscape Mapping
phases.description: For any condition, map the complete treatment landscape: standard of care, emerging therapies, experimental treatments, immunotherapy options, genetic/targeted therapy, clinical trials, and where AI is being used in treatment planning.
tasks: Standard of care: NCCN guidelines, UpToDate, MDCalc protocols
Emerging therapies: papers published in last 12 months showing significant results
Clinical trials: active trials by phase (I, II, III), location, eligibility
Immunotherapy: checkpoint inhibitors, CAR-T, tumor vaccines for this condition
Genetic markers: which genetic tests could guide treatment selection
AI in treatment planning: radiation planning AI, drug interaction AI, dosage optimization
Cost and access: treatment costs in Canada, provincial coverage, compassionate use programs
Create visual treatment decision tree
phases.estimatedCost: API costs minimal — mostly research compilation
workarounds: Date-stamp everything
Focus on Canadian availability first
Weekly automated refresh for active clinical trials

phases.name: Automated Condition Monitor
phases.description: Set up automated monitoring: when a new paper is published, new clinical trial opens, or breakthrough is announced for a tracked condition, get notified immediately.
tasks: PubMed alerts: automated search for new papers on condition keywords
ClinicalTrials.gov watch: new trials matching condition + location
Google Scholar alerts: citation tracking for key papers
News monitoring: AI scans health news for breakthrough announcements
Weekly digest: AI summarizes all new developments
Urgency detection: flag potential breakthroughs vs routine publications
Integration with AI-Progress dashboard: tracked conditions as a data file
phases.estimatedCost: Free (PubMed alerts free, Scholar alerts free, scheduled tasks free)
blockers: Information overload — many papers are incremental
AI may over-flag routine publications as breakthroughs
workarounds: AI filters by impact factor, citation count, and novelty
Human review of weekly digest
Configurable urgency thresholds

biggestRisk: Medical misinformation — AI hallucinations in a medical context could be harmful
mitigation: NEVER present as medical advice. Always cite primary sources. Cross-verify with multiple AIs. Include 'discuss with your doctor' on every output. Focus on being a research AID that helps you ask better questions.

name: Full AI Feature Film: 60-Minute Movie Production
description: Push beyond short films. Produce a complete 60-minute feature using AI tools for every stage. This is the hardest creative challenge in AI right now. Two AI shorts made Sundance 2026, but nobody has done a full hour of coherent, watchable AI film. We document exactly where the technology breaks and build workarounds.
currentState: 60-second AI films are achievable (2-3 hours work). 5-minute shorts are hard but proven. Character consistency, temporal coherence, and narrative structure are the unsolved problems at feature length.
targetState: A watchable 60-minute film produced 90%+ by AI tools, with documented time, cost, and quality at each stage. Published on YouTube. Honest quality assessment — not claiming it's Hollywood, documenting what AI can and can't do at feature length.

phases.name: Capability Assessment: What's the Longest Coherent AI Video Today?
phases.description: Before attempting 60 minutes, we need to find the actual ceiling. What's the longest piece of coherent AI-generated video that exists? What techniques were used? Where does coherence break?
tasks: Research: find the longest existing AI-generated videos with consistent characters
Analyze Sundance 2026 AI films: techniques, tools, human intervention points
Test Runway Gen-4 maximum coherent clip length (currently 4-16 seconds per generation)
Test Kling maximum coherent clip length
Test character consistency: how many consecutive scenes can maintain the same character?
Test scene transitions: can AI handle cuts, dissolves, camera angle changes?
Document exact failure points with screenshots and timestamps
Publish findings as capability report in dashboard
phases.estimatedCost: Free tiers for initial testing. ~$30-60 in subscriptions for thorough testing.
blockers: Current max clip length is 4-16 seconds per generation
workarounds: Segment approach: treat each scene as independent, maintain consistency through art style
Use LoRA/style references aggressively
Limit character close-ups where drift is most visible

phases.name: Narrative Design for AI Limitations
phases.description: Design a story specifically built around AI limitations. Narration-driven, episodic structure, art style that masks inconsistency, minimal dialogue. The script IS the workaround.
tasks: Story format: anthology (3-4 loosely connected 15-min segments)
Narration-heavy: avoid lip-sync entirely
Art style: stylized illustration or anime (not photorealistic — too unforgiving)
Character design: simple, distinctive silhouettes (easier to maintain across scenes)
Scene structure: short cuts (3-5 sec), montage-heavy, atmospheric
Script written by AI, reviewed and edited by human
Storyboard every scene with Midjourney before any video generation
Estimated 400-600 shots for 60 minutes
phases.estimatedCost: Midjourney: ~$14 CAD/mo. AI writing: free tiers.
blockers: 400+ shots is a massive organizational challenge
Story quality matters — bad script = bad movie regardless of visuals
workarounds: Project management tool for shot tracking
Multiple script drafts with AI peer review
Test audience feedback after first segment before committing to full film

phases.name: Production: Generate, Assemble, Polish
phases.description: Generate all 400-600 video clips. Full audio production. Assembly editing. Color grading. Sound design.
tasks: Batch generation: Midjourney for all keyframes (2-3 days of generation time)
Video generation: Runway/Kling for all clips (1-2 weeks of generation + iteration)
Voice: ElevenLabs for all narration and character voices
Music: Suno for full 60-minute score (multiple tracks for different moods)
Sound design: layered ambient audio, foley, transitions
Assembly: DaVinci Resolve for timeline management at feature length
Color grading: Colourlab AI for consistent look across segments
Quality control: review every shot, regenerate failures
Estimated total production time: 2-4 weeks
phases.estimatedCost: $50-150 in tool subscriptions over 1-2 months
blockers: Storage: 60min of footage + source files = hundreds of GB
workarounds: Batch processing scripts to automate generation queues
Version control for all assets
Regular quality checkpoints every 15 minutes of assembled footage

phases.name: Publish and Document
phases.description: Publish the finished film. Document the entire process: time, cost, tools used, where human intervention was needed, quality assessment, and honest review of what AI filmmaking can and can't do in 2026.
tasks: Final export: 1080p or 4K
Upload to YouTube
Create behind-the-scenes documentary showing the AI production process
Detailed blog post: tools, techniques, costs, time breakdown
Side-by-side comparison: AI output vs what a human team could produce
Community feedback collection
Capability report update in AI-Progress dashboard
Plan for next attempt with improved tools (re-attempt in 6 months)
phases.estimatedCost: Free (YouTube is free, writing is free)
blockers: Copyright concerns with AI-generated content on YouTube
Quality may not meet watchability threshold
workarounds: YouTube allows AI content with disclosure
If full film quality is too low, publish as 'AI filmmaking experiment' documentary format
Be honest about quality — the process documentation IS the value

biggestRisk: It might not be watchable. 60 minutes of AI-generated video might be unwatchable no matter how good the tools are in March 2026.
mitigation: Anthology format — each 15-minute segment stands alone. If the full 60 minutes doesn't work, 2-3 good 15-minute shorts still work. Fail gracefully.
